questions.question: In the legacy pattern (before Vue 3.5), what must be true for a template ref to work?
options: The ref must be declared with a capital letter
The variable name in script must exactly match the ref attribute string in the template
The element must have an id attribute matching the ref name
The ref must be declared inside onMounted

questions.question: What does useTemplateRef() in Vue 3.5+ improve over the old ref() pattern?
options: It automatically focuses the element after mount
It decouples the variable name from the ref attribute, improving IDE support and avoiding silent null bugs
It makes the template ref reactive to DOM attribute changes
It provides automatic cleanup without needing onUnmounted

questions.question: When placing a template ref on a child component using <script setup>, what do you get by default?
options: The child's entire reactive state object
Nothing — <script setup> components expose nothing by default; use defineExpose()
A proxy to all public methods and data
